figures.caption: Figure 4 Performance for different RoPE θ values and training methods (CE vs. KD) across Needle in a Haystack (left) and RULER (right).
Figure 5 Comparison of the average pre-training performance (left, in %) and training loss across different θ settings (right) for KD and CE objectives.

Figure 7 Distance between position index 0 and subsequent positions for a single hidden-dimension element in the query hidden state, measured before (Pre-RoPE) and after (Post-RoPE) applying rotary positional embeddings.
Figure 12 Euclidean distance between Phase 1 and Phase 2 pre-trained hidden states per sequence element for Layer 0 (top) and Layer 30 (bottom).
Figure 11 Euclidean distance between Phase 1 and Phase 2 pre-trained hidden states per hidden-state element for Layer 0 (top) and Layer 30 (bottom).

Figure 2 (Top) Each dimension pair undergoes incremental rotation as position m increases, encoded by a unique rotation angle mθi. (Center) Different dimension pairs rotate at different frequencies: low-index pairs distinguish nearby tokens, while high-index pairs encode long-range positional relationships. (Bottom) The base parameter θ controls how quickly angles accumulate: a smaller θ causes faster wrapping (limiting context length), while a larger θ spreads rotations across longer sequences.
Figure 8 Cosine similarity between the query hidden state at position index 0 and subsequent positions, measured before (Pre-RoPE) and after (Post-RoPE) applying rotary positional embeddings.

Figure 9 Per-layer cosine similarity between position index 0 and subsequent position indices (offset by 2,048 tokens each). Each curve represents a different layer, showing progressive amplification of positional divergence through the model.
Table 1 Student and Teacher Model Specifications
Figure 10 Output layer top-10 token ranking and probability mass distribution across sequence positions. Letters represent token indices and their rank.
Figure 3 Schematic visualization of scaled and unscaled RoPE θ in the short-context (Phase 1) and long-context (Phase 2) pre-training stages.
